evals.prompt: I need to create a project.yaml config for a new Go microservice called 'order-service'. It should have a REST API generated from OpenAPI spec (api.swagger.yml), a Kafka producer that sends order_created and order_updated events with typed messages from JSON Schema, and a sys endpoint for metrics. The git repo is github.com/mycompany/order-service. Please write the complete project.yaml.
evals.expected_output: A valid project.yaml with REST ogen transport, sys template transport, jsonschema section, kafka producer with schema references, and an application that uses all of them. Must follow all validation rules.

assertions.name: has_main_section
assertions.description: Config has main section with name: order-service, logger, registry_type

assertions.name: has_git_section
assertions.description: Config has git section with repo and module_path

assertions.name: has_ogen_rest
assertions.description: Has REST transport with generator_type: ogen and path to swagger file

assertions.name: has_sys_transport
assertions.description: Has sys transport with generator_type: template and generator_template: sys

assertions.name: has_jsonschema
assertions.description: Has jsonschema section with schemas for typed Kafka messages

assertions.name: has_kafka_producer
assertions.description: Has kafka section with type: producer and events referencing jsonschema

assertions.name: has_application
assertions.description: Has applications section with transport in object format [{name: x}] and kafka reference

assertions.name: transport_object_format
assertions.description: Transport uses object format (- name: x) not string format (- x)

assertions.name: all_entities_used
assertions.description: All defined rest, kafka entities are referenced in applications

evals.prompt: I'm getting error: rest 'api' is not used in any application. Config has rest api + sys, worker notifier, but application only has sys transport and notifier worker.
evals.expected_output: Identifies that 'api' REST transport is not listed in any application's transport array. Fix: add {name: api} to the server application's transport list.

assertions.name: identifies_missing_transport
assertions.description: Correctly identifies that 'api' is not in any application's transport list

assertions.name: provides_fix
assertions.description: Shows the corrected config with api added to application transport

assertions.name: uses_object_format
assertions.description: Fix uses object format [{name: api}] not string format

evals.prompt: Add Telegram bot worker to existing REST API project as separate application/container
evals.expected_output: Updated config with: worker section (telegram template), driver section (telegram driver with correct import/package/obj_name), new bot application with sys transport + worker + driver with params

assertions.name: has_worker_section
assertions.description: Adds worker with generator_type: template, generator_template: telegram

assertions.name: has_driver_section
assertions.description: Adds driver with name, import, package, obj_name for telegram

assertions.name: has_bot_application
assertions.description: Creates separate bot application with sys transport, telegram worker, and telegram driver

assertions.name: driver_import_correct
assertions.description: Driver import uses the project's module_path (github.com/myorg/myapp/pkg/drivers/telegram)

assertions.name: original_preserved
assertions.description: Original server application and REST config are preserved unchanged

assertions.name: driver_params
assertions.description: Bot application's driver has params like WithUpdatePoller()
